title: Dashboard enhancements and improvements
body:
* **Dashboard embedding for external users is generally available**: Embed AI/BI dashboards in external applications without requiring viewers to have Databricks accounts. See [What is embedding for external users?](/aws/en/dashboards/share/embedding/external-embed).
* **Hide Databricks logo in embedded dashboards**: Externally embedded dashboards now support hiding the Databricks logo using the `hideDatabricksLogo` option. See [Hide the Databricks logo](/aws/en/dashboards/share/embedding/external-embed#hide-logo).
* **Metric view lineage highlighting**: Hovering over metric views in the lineage pane now highlights the visualizations built on those metric views.
* **Certification and deprecation icons in lineage**: Data objects in the dashboard lineage pane now show their certification or deprecation status.
* **Clear individual active filters**: You can now remove a single filter selection from the active filter bar without resetting all active filters.
* **Mobile layouts**: Dashboards now automatically render in a mobile-friendly layout when viewed on small screens in Genie One. See [Mobile layout](/aws/en/dashboards/concepts#mobile-layout).
* **Custom subscription email subjects**: When configuring a dashboard subscription, authors can now edit the subject line of notification emails sent to subscribers.
* **Accounting-style number formatting**: Number fields now support accounting-style formatting, which displays negative values in parentheses and vertically aligns currency symbols. See [Format numeric values](/aws/en/dashboards/manage/visualizations#number-formatting).
* **Custom fonts**: Dashboard authors can now select custom fonts for dashboards. Fonts must be preinstalled on viewer machines. If a font is not available, dashboards fall back to Databricks default fonts. See [Set a custom font](/aws/en/dashboards/manage/settings#custom-font).
* **Genie Code for dashboard authoring (Public Preview)**: Automate multi-step dashboard workflows using natural language prompts to create datasets, visualizations, layouts, and filters. See [Use Genie Code for dashboard authoring](/aws/en/dashboards/manage/dashboard-agent).
